questions.q: Chandra Shekhar served as Prime Minister of India during which period?
questions.explanation: Chandra Shekhar served as Prime Minister from November 1990 to June 1991.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar is given which descriptive title on the Katha Cards deck?
questions.explanation: He is titled 'The Young Turk' on the deck.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar is known for which long march undertaken in 1983?
questions.explanation: He is known for his 1983 Bharat Yatra, a long march across the country on foot.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's 1983 Bharat Yatra was a march carried out in what manner?
questions.explanation: The Bharat Yatra was a long march across the country on foot.

questions.q: In which month and year did Chandra Shekhar become Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He became Prime Minister in November 1990.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's government came to power as which kind of grouping?
options: A breakaway faction with outside support
questions.explanation: He led a breakaway faction that governed with outside support.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's tenure as Prime Minister is best described as which of the following?
options: A short tenure of a few months
questions.explanation: His tenure lasted only a few months, from November 1990 to June 1991.

questions.q: In which year did Chandra Shekhar undertake his famous Bharat Yatra?
questions.explanation: He undertook the Bharat Yatra in 1983.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's term as Prime Minister ended in which month and year?
questions.explanation: His term as Prime Minister ended in June 1991.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar became Prime Minister immediately after which leader's tenure?
questions.explanation: Chandra Shekhar became Prime Minister in 1990, succeeding V.P. Singh.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar is often described as which sequential Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Chandra Shekhar is counted as the eighth Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: Which Prime Minister succeeded Chandra Shekhar in office in 1991?
questions.explanation: P.V. Narasimha Rao succeeded Chandra Shekhar as Prime Minister in 1991.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's premiership fell within which broader phase of Indian politics?
options: The era of coalition and minority governments around 1990
questions.explanation: His premiership came in the era of coalition and minority governments around 1990.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's government governed with outside support from which broad direction?
options: From a party outside his own faction
questions.explanation: His breakaway faction governed with outside support from a party outside his own group.

questions.q: The Bharat Yatra associated with Chandra Shekhar is best characterised as what?
options: A state election campaign by air
questions.explanation: The Bharat Yatra was a nationwide march on foot undertaken in 1983.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's tenure as Prime Minister spanned which calendar years?
questions.explanation: His tenure spanned late 1990 into 1991.

questions.q: Before becoming Prime Minister, Chandra Shekhar was long active in which arena of public life?
questions.explanation: He was long active in parliamentary and opposition politics before becoming Prime Minister.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's government is counted among which type of governments in this period?
questions.explanation: It is counted among the short-lived governments of the 1990-1991 period.

questions.q: The Bharat Yatra is often cited as evidence of Chandra Shekhar's emphasis on what?
options: Connecting with the country at the grassroots
questions.explanation: The march is cited as evidence of his emphasis on connecting with the country at the grassroots.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's premiership directly preceded the start of which period in 1991?
questions.explanation: His premiership directly preceded the economic liberalisation reforms that began in 1991.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar formally assumed the premiership through which constitutional act?
options: Election by the public to the post
questions.explanation: He was appointed Prime Minister by the President, as the Constitution requires.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's brief government and that of his immediate predecessor were both examples of what?
questions.explanation: Both his government and V.P. Singh's depended on outside support, typical of the period.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's tenure as Prime Minister is notable for being among the shortest, lasting roughly how long?
questions.explanation: His tenure from November 1990 to June 1991 lasted roughly seven months.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's premiership came between the tenures of which two Prime Ministers?
options: P.V. Narasimha Rao and Deve Gowda
V.P. Singh and P.V. Narasimha Rao
questions.explanation: His premiership came between those of V.P. Singh and P.V. Narasimha Rao.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's term ended in 1991, the same year that began which major economic shift in India?
options: The nationalisation of the Reserve Bank
questions.explanation: His term ended in 1991, the year the economic liberalisation reforms began under his successor.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's government exemplifies which feature of Indian parliamentary practice?
options: A minority government reliant on outside support
A government with a single-party supermajority
questions.explanation: It exemplifies a minority government reliant on outside support in the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: A Prime Minister such as Chandra Shekhar, leading a government without a single-party majority, must above all retain what to remain in office?
options: The confidence of the Lok Sabha
A two-thirds majority in the Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: Under the Constitution, the government must retain the confidence of the Lok Sabha to remain in office.

questions.q: The 1983 Bharat Yatra associated with Chandra Shekhar is best understood as part of which kind of political activity?
options: Mass political outreach across the country
An election conducted by the Election Commission
questions.explanation: The Bharat Yatra was a form of mass political outreach across the country.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar's premiership is positioned within which phase of national governance described on the deck as 'the era of coalitions'?
options: The late-1980s-to-1990s phase of coalition and minority governments
questions.explanation: His premiership falls within the late-1980s-to-1990s phase of coalition and minority governments.

questions.q: Chandra Shekhar is the second of three Prime Ministers with brief tenures clustered around which two years?
questions.explanation: His brief tenure is clustered with others around 1989 to 1991 during this unstable period.
